methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
MaverickX is a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Eric Herrera that has raised $19M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public peer (LNZA) in the climate and energy sector, with particular emphasis on three same-sector companies at Series A stage: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $19M raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a valuation of $48M. Peer-median analysis across the three Series A comparables yields a median valuation of $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. The peer-median approach suggests a higher valuation reflecting the later-stage positioning of the comp set relative to MaverickX's current seed stage.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $40M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $99M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty about whether MaverickX will achieve Series A milestones comparable to the peer cohort. Key variables include product-market validation, capital efficiency relative to peer burn rates, and competitive positioning within the energy transition landscape. The peer-median skews high due to stage mismatch; a seed-stage discount to the peer median would narrow the defensible range toward the $40M–$60M band absent evidence of exceptional traction.
